title: Basic Foundation
description: Sign rules, exponent laws, algebraic identities — the building blocks of all mathematics.

title: Story of Symbols
description: Understand what π, e, log, ln, Σ, ∫, √, and ∞ actually mean through stories and visual sandbox labs.

title: Number Systems
description: Natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real numbers — understand each type clearly.

title: Real Number System
description: Properties of real numbers, completeness, and the number line explained simply.

title: Logarithms
description: Understand log properties: product, quotient, and power rules.

title: Sets
description: Introduction to set theory, unions, intersections, and venn diagrams.

title: Relations and Functions
description: How inputs map to outputs — domain, range, types of functions, and graphing basics.

title: Binary Operations
description: Operations on sets, closure, commutativity, associativity, and identity elements.

title: Logic
description: Propositions, truth tables, logical connectives, and valid arguments explained clearly.

title: Combinatorics
description: Counting principles, pigeonhole principle, and inclusion-exclusion made simple.

title: Permutations and Combinations
description: Arrangements vs selections — nPr and nCr with clear examples.

title: Mathematical Induction
description: Prove statements for all natural numbers using base case and inductive step.

title: Sequences and Series
description: Arithmetic and geometric progressions, summation formulas, and convergence basics.

title: Trigonometry
description: Sin, cos, tan — ratios, identities, unit circle, and solving triangles visually.

title: Complex Numbers
description: What is i? Argand plane, modulus, argument, and operations on complex numbers.

title: Equations
description: Linear, quadratic, and polynomial equations — solving methods with step-by-step approach.

title: Inequalities
description: Solving and graphing linear, quadratic, and absolute value inequalities.

title: Matrices
description: Types, operations, transpose, and matrix algebra explained with grid visuals.

title: Determinants
description: Calculating determinants, properties, cofactors, and Cramer's rule step by step.

title: Applications of Matrices & Determinants
description: Solving systems of equations, inverse matrices, and rank applications.

title: Vectors
description: Magnitude, direction, dot product, cross product, and vector applications.

title: Three Dimensional Geometry
description: Lines, planes, and distances in 3D space with coordinate geometry.

title: Differential Calculus
description: Limits, derivatives, and differentiation rules — the foundation of calculus.

title: Applications of Differential Calculus
description: Maxima, minima, rate of change, tangent lines, and curve sketching.

title: Integration
description: Antiderivatives, definite integrals, substitution, and integration techniques.

title: Applications of Integral Calculus
description: Area under curves, volume of revolution, and real-world integral applications.

title: Linear Differential Equations
description: First and second order linear ODEs, integrating factors, and solution methods.

title: Exact Equations
description: Identifying and solving exact differential equations with clear conditions.

title: Applications of Differential Equations
description: Growth, decay, Newton's law of cooling, and circuit problems using ODEs.

title: Linear Programming
description: Optimization, constraints, feasible region, and graphical method step by step.

title: Statistics
description: Mean, median, mode, variance, standard deviation — data summarized simply.

title: Correlation and Regression
description: Measuring relationships between variables, scatter plots, and line of best fit.

title: Probability
description: Events, sample space, conditional probability, and Bayes' theorem basics.

title: Discrete Probability Distributions
description: Binomial, Poisson, and other discrete distributions with worked examples.

title: Statistical Estimation
description: Point estimates, confidence intervals, and hypothesis testing fundamentals.

title: Differential Geometry
description: Curvature, torsion, and properties of curves and surfaces in space.
